name: Enterprise Readable

sans.name: Inter
sans.rationale: A neutral humanist-grotesque sans with a large x-height, open apertures, and excellent legibility at small UI sizes; ships with tabular and slashed-zero OpenType features useful for data.

mono.name: JetBrains Mono
mono.role: Code, identifiers, log output, fixed-width data
mono.rationale: A monospace with clearly differentiated 0/O, 1/l/I, and generous spacing for unambiguous reading of codes and code.

display.name: Inter
display.rationale: Kept in the same family at heavier weights and tighter tracking to maximise consistency and minimise font payload; no separate display face is needed for an enterprise tool.

scale.ratio: 1.2 (minor third), tightened at small UI sizes
scale.notes: Body copy sits at base (16px); dense tables may use sm (14px) but never below xs (12px) for readable text. Captions and metadata use xs.

line_heights.normal: 1.5 (body text, meeting WCAG 1.4.8 guidance of at least 1.5)

fallback_stacks.sans: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
fallback_stacks.mono: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace

localisation_notes: Inter covers Latin, Cyrillic, and Greek; for CJK, Arabic, or Devanagari pair with Noto Sans variants and verify line-height increases needed for those scripts
Enable tabular-nums (font-variant-numeric) on all numeric columns so figures align and do not reflow as values change
Use slashed or dotted zero for codes and identifiers to avoid 0/O confusion
Allow line-height to grow for scripts with tall ascenders/descenders rather than clipping
evidence: Inter is purpose-built for screen UI (rsms.me/inter) with documented tabular and slashed-zero features
WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.8 recommends line spacing of at least 1.5x within paragraphs for readability
Tabular figures are a standard requirement for financial and data tables to preserve column alignment
